rulings.Case Title: OG TREE, INC. VS. OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2021 line 1. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare order and judgment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LSC 906-908 BROADWAY LLC VS. STARTUP TEMPLE HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of Receiver

PLAINTIFF LSC 906-908 BROADWAY LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of Receiver is continued to June 17, 2021. rulings.Case Title: YULANDA D.A. WILLIAMS VS. REGINOLD P. DANIELS SR. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANTS NAJUAWANDA DANIELS and REGINOLD DANIELS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) allege facts, not conclusions, regarding forcible entry/detainer (threats, menace, etc); and to (2) reconcile allegations in paragraphs 30, 32 and the letter rescinding the license effective March 1, 2021. The Court notes that complaint was filed on March 2, 2021, one day after the effective date of the revocation of license. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
